name: Boar Hildisvini
description: Hildisvini, meaning 'battle swine' or 'battle boar,' is a golden boar in Norse mythology owned by the goddess Freyja. This magnificent creature served as Freyja's mount when she didn't use her chariot pulled by cats. Hildisvini is described as having bristles of gold that shone brilliantly, illuminating the darkness. The boar's creation is attributed to the dwarves Dáin and Náinn, commissioned by Loki to mock the god Thor by creating treasures that could rival the works of the sons of Ivaldi, who crafted Odin's spear Gungnir, Freyr's ship Skidbladnir, and Sif's golden hair. While not possessing overt magical abilities beyond its radiant glow, Hildisvini symbolizes Freyja's connection to nature, fertility, and battle. The boar represents courage, strength, and prosperity, reflecting Freyja's multifaceted nature as a goddess. Its golden bristles are often interpreted as representing the sun's life-giving energy and the abundance of the harvest, linking Hildisvini to agricultural cycles and the continuation of life. In some accounts, it is theorized that Hildisvini may be a transformed human, specifically Óttar, a human Freyja favored, further intertwining the animal with themes of transformation and divine favor. Possessing Hildisvini signified great status and power, reflecting the owner's connection to the divine and the natural world.
subtitle: Freyja's Golden Battle Swine
origin: Crafted by the dwarves Dáin and Náinn
currentLocation: Unknown, presumed to be with Freyja

keyMyths.title: The Creation of Hildisvini
keyMyths.description: Hildisvini was created by the dwarves Dáin and Náinn as part of Loki's scheme to mock the gods' craftsmanship. The golden boar was intended to rival the creations of the sons of Ivaldi.

keyMyths.title: Freyja's Mount
keyMyths.description: Hildisvini served as Freyja's mount, especially when she chose not to use her cat-drawn chariot. The boar's golden bristles illuminated her path.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A primary source for Norse mythology, including accounts of Freyja and the creation of Hildisvini.
sources.text: A primary source for Norse mythology, including accounts of Freyja and the creation of Hildisvini.

sources.author: Unknown
sources.description: A collection of Old Norse poems, containing references to Hildisvini in connection with Freyja.
